pi_items.source: export_result.sh queries and verifier.py. Note: The names in the directive (Michael Taylor, James Thomas, William White, Ethan Martin) explicitly contradict the synthetic DB (Ashley Johnson, Matthew Garcia, Stephanie Brown, Brian Taylor) and their roles are HR/Marketing/Finance rather than 'Production staff'.

privileged_info_summary: I audited the synthetic Sentrifugo environment and verified the expected status 'Furloughed - Unpaid', leave type 'Annual Leave', and announcement title 'Plant Operations Pause'. Crucially, I discovered that the directive text claims the target employees are 'Production staff' named Michael Taylor, James Thomas, William White, and Ethan Martin, but the actual Sentrifugo DB is seeded with completely different names and departments for these IDs: EMP008 is Ashley Johnson (HR), EMP011 is Matthew Garcia (Marketing), EMP014 is Stephanie Brown (Customer Success), and EMP017 is Brian Taylor (Finance). The verifier strictly checks the employee IDs regardless of these contradictions.
